1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Google OAuth - Service account JWT authentication
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGO_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbgo_*)
29	
30	zrbgo_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo already kindled"
32	
33	  # Validate required tools (rehomed from rbl_Locator.sh)
34	  command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "openssl not found - required for JWT signing and encoding"
35	  command -v curl    >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "curl not found - required for OAuth exchange"
36	  command -v jq      >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "jq not found - required for JSON parsing"
37	
38	  buc_log_args "Ensure RBGC is kindled first"
39	  zrbgc_sentinel
40	
41	  buc_log_args "Check environment"
42	  zburd_sentinel
43	
44	  buc_log_args "Set Module Variables (ZRBGO_*)"
45	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_header.json"
46	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_claims.json"
47	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_unsigned.txt"
48	  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_signature.txt"
49	  readonly ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_oauth_response.json"
50	  readonly ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_openssl_stderr.txt"
51	  readonly ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_curl_stderr.txt"
52	  readonly ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jq_stderr.txt"
53	  readonly ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_consumer_retry_body.json"
54	
55	  readonly ZRBGO_KINDLED=1
56	}
57	
58	zrbgo_sentinel() {
59	  test "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgo not kindled - call zrbgo_kindle first"
60	}
61	
62	######################################################################
63	# Base64 primitives — generic openssl wrappers.
64	#
65	# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of
66	# kindle order. rbgo base64url-encodes JWT material; rbgg/rbgp decode SA
67	# private keys and rbfd decodes Cloud Build step outputs. The -A flag
68	# (suppress line wrapping) is load-bearing on every site and lives only
69	# here, so it cannot be silently dropped on one path.
70	
71	rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file() {
72	  local -r z_b64="${1:-}"
73	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
74	  test -n "${z_b64}"    || return 1
75	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
76	  printf '%s' "${z_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_output}" || return 1
77	}
78	
79	rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file() {
80	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
81	  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
82	  test -n "${z_input}"  || return 1
83	  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
84	  test -f "${z_input}"  || return 1
85	  openssl enc -base64 -d -A < "${z_input}" > "${z_output}" || return 1
86	}
87	
88	rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture() {
89	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
90	  printf '%s' "${z_input}" | openssl enc -base64 -A
91	}
92	
93	rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture() {
94	  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
95	  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
96	  openssl enc -base64 -A < "${z_file}"
97	}
98	
99	# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.
100	rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate() {
101	  case "${1:-}" in
102	    7|28|35|56) return 0 ;;
103	    *)          return 1 ;;
104	  esac
105	}
106	
107	zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture() {
108	  zrbgo_sentinel
109	
110	  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
111	
112	  # Base64url: encode, then URL-safe transform and strip = padding
113	  local z_encoded
114	  z_encoded=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "${z_input}") || {
115	    buc_warn "openssl base64url encode failed"
116	    return 1
117	  }
118	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//+/-}"
119	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//\//_}"
120	  z_encoded="${z_encoded//=/}"
121	  printf '%s' "${z_encoded}"
122	}
123	
124	zrbgo_build_jwt_capture() {
125	  zrbgo_sentinel
126	
127	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
128	
129	  # RBRA_* expected: CLIENT_EMAIL, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC
130	  # RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY is sourced as a multi-line string (real newlines from PEM).
131	  # printf '%b' below is defensive and tolerates either real-newline or '\n'-escape
132	  # form; PEM keys contain no backslashes so %b is otherwise a no-op.
133	  # Only source if not already loaded (avoids readonly conflict in subshells)
134	  if test -z "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"; then
135	    buc_log_args "Source RBRA file"
136	    source "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
137	  fi
138	
139	  buc_log_args "Validate required variables"
140	  test -n "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"       || return 1
141	  test -n "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"        || return 1
142	  test -n "${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC:-}" || return 1
143	
144	  buc_log_args "Build JWT header"
145	  printf '%s' '{"alg":"RS256","typ":"JWT"}' > "${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}"
146	
147	  buc_log_args "Calculate timestamps"
148	  local z_now
149	  z_now=$(date +%s) || return 1
150	  local -r z_exp=$((z_now + RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC))
151	
152	  buc_log_args "Build JWT claims"
153	  jq -n                                         \
154	    --arg iss   "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}"          \
155	    --arg scope "${RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM}"  \
156	    --arg aud   "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"       \
157	    --argjson iat "${z_now}"                    \
158	    --argjson exp "${z_exp}"                    \
159	    '{iss: $iss, scope: $scope, aud: $aud, iat: $iat, exp: $exp}' \
160	    > "${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}" || return 1
161	
162	  buc_log_args "Encode header and claims"
163	  local z_header_enc
164	  z_header_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}")") || return 1
165	
166	  local z_claims_enc
167	  z_claims_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}")") || return 1
168	
169	  buc_log_args "Create unsigned JWT"
170	  printf '%s' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}" > "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}"
171	
172	  buc_log_args "Sign with RSA256 (without writing key to disk)"
173	  # Convert literal \n sequences to real newlines via printf %b and feed via process substitution
174	  openssl dgst -sha256 \
175	    -sign <(printf '%b' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}\n") \
176	    -out "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}" \
177	    "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}" || {
178	      buc_warn "openssl RSA256 sign failed: $(<"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}")"
179	      return 1
180	    }
181	
182	  buc_log_args "Base64url encode signature"
183	  local z_signature
184	  z_signature=$(rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}") || {
185	    buc_warn "openssl signature base64 encode failed"
186	    return 1
187	  }
188	  z_signature="${z_signature//+/-}"
189	  z_signature="${z_signature//\//_}"
190	  z_signature="${z_signature//=/}"
191	
192	  buc_log_args "Return complete JWT"
193	  printf '%s\n' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}.${z_signature}"
194	}
195	
196	zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture() {
197	  zrbgo_sentinel
198	
199	  local -r z_jwt="$1"
200	
201	  local z_attempt=0
202	  local z_curl_status=0
203	  while :; do
204	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
205	
206	    buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
207	    z_curl_status=0
208	    curl -sS -X POST "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"                                        \
209	      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}"                           \
210	      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"                                         \
211	      -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"                           \
212	      -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=${z_jwt}" \
213	      > "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}" || z_curl_status=$?
214	
215	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 && break
216	
217	    buc_warn "OAuth curl exit ${z_curl_status}: $(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")"
218	    rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}" || return 1
219	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" || return 1
220	
221	    buc_step "OAuth token mint: transient curl ${z_curl_status}, retry ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
222	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
223	  done
224	
225	  # Scrubber filters by field NAME, not value. Deliberate best-effort log hygiene:
226	  # explicitly deletes access_token/refresh_token, then drops any field whose key
227	  # matches token|secret|key|password (case-insensitive) — catches id_token,
228	  # client_secret, etc. If the OAuth provider ever returns a new secret-carrying
229	  # field whose name doesn't match this regex, the scrub would miss it; update
230	  # the regex here when that happens.
231	  buc_log_args "Debug: Show the actual response (minus secrets)"
232	  jq 'del(.access_token, .refresh_token)
233	      | with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
234	    "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" | buc_log_pipe || buc_log_args "OAuth response parsing failed"
235	
236	  buc_log_args "OAuth token exchange completed"
237	
238	  buc_log_args "Extract access token"
239	  local z_token
240	  z_token=$(jq -r '.access_token // empty' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}") || return 1
241	  test -n "${z_token}" || {
242	    buc_warn "OAuth response missing access_token; redacted response body follows:"
243	    jq 'with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
244	      "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" >&2 \
245	      || printf '%s\n' "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")" >&2
246	    return 1
247	  }
248	
249	  printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
250	}
251	
252	######################################################################
253	# External Functions (rbgo_*)
254	
255	rbgo_get_token_capture() {
256	  zrbgo_sentinel
257	
258	  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"
259	
260	  buc_doc_brief "Exchange service account credentials for OAuth2 access token (retries on JWT-propagation race)"
261	  buc_doc_param "rbra_file" "Path to RBRA credentials file containing RBRA_* variables"
262	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
263	
264	  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
265	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
266	
267	  local z_attempt=0
268	  local z_elapsed=0
269	  local z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
270	  local z_jwt=""
271	  local z_token=""
272	  local z_status=0
273	  local z_err=""
274	  local z_err_desc=""
275	
276	  while :; do
277	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
278	
279	    z_status=0
280	    z_jwt=$(zrbgo_build_jwt_capture "${z_rbra_file}") || z_status=$?
281	    test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || return 1
282	
283	    z_status=0
284	    z_token=$(zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture "${z_jwt}") || z_status=$?
285	
286	    if test "${z_status}" -eq 0 && test -n "${z_token}"; then
287	      printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
288	      return 0
289	    fi
290	
291	    # TEMP-FORENSIC ₢BBABL: capture every token-endpoint mint failure to a
292	    # stable, append-only absolute log so a later successful mint cannot clobber
293	    # the failing body (the memo's Host-B capture gap, memo-20260527). Purpose:
294	    # confirm the ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID token-endpoint shape before writing the
295	    # touchpoint-2 match. REMOVE once the shape is captured and the match lands.
296	    { printf '=== rbgo mint fail rbra=%s status=%s ===\n' "${z_rbra_file}" "${z_status}"
297	      printf '%s' "$(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")" "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")"
298	      printf '\n=== end ===\n'
299	    } >> /tmp/rbgo_BBABL_forensic.log 2>/dev/null || true
300	
301	    # Discriminate failure: only the SA-propagation race shapes retry —
302	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature.`         (fresh-key propagation lag)
303	    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid grant: account not found` (fresh-SA propagation lag)
304	    # Any other shape fails fast.
305	    z_err=$(jq -r '.error // ""'             "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err=""
306	    z_err_desc=$(jq -r '.error_description // ""' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err_desc=""
307	
308	    test "${z_err}" = "invalid_grant" || return 1
309	    case "${z_err_desc}" in
310	      "Invalid JWT Signature."|"Invalid grant: account not found") ;;
311	      *) return 1 ;;
312	    esac
313	
314	    cp "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" "${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}" || return 1
315	
316	    if test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC}"; then
317	      buc_warn "Token mint: SA propagation race timeout after ${z_elapsed}s (${z_attempt} attempts); last body: ${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}"
318	      return 1
319	    fi
320	
321	    buc_step "Token mint: attempt ${z_attempt} hit SA propagation race (${z_err_desc}) at ${z_elapsed}s, sleep ${z_delay}s"
322	    sleep "${z_delay}"
323	    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + z_delay))
324	    z_delay=$((z_delay * 2))
325	    test "${z_delay}" -le "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}"
326	  done
327	}
328	
329	# Authenticate the host docker client to a GAR registry with bounded retry on
330	# the moby/moby#44350 premature-timeout transient (see
331	# RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE). docker login is the lone login/pull/push
332	# verb with no internal retry and a hardcoded 15s daemon->registry auth timeout;
333	# against a healthy-but-slow backend it fails where the endpoint is in fact fine.
334	# This mirrors the curl-transient tolerance in rbgu_http_json: retry only the
335	# surveyed signature, fail fast on everything else (real auth failures emit
336	# "unauthorized" and do not match), so clean-failure semantics are preserved.
337	# Stateless (no sentinel) — safe to call from any module regardless of kindle
338	# order, like rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate.
339	#
340	# A second Palisade neighbor lives here for headless Cygwin: Docker Desktop's
341	# wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for
342	# (RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE), and Windows docker ignores an empty
343	# credsStore, so config alone cannot divert the store to the file store. On
344	# that exact signature the function bends ONCE: auth already succeeded, so it
345	# writes the credential straight into docker's base64 file store at
346	# ${HOME}/.docker/config.json (the `auths` map, the form WSL uses) and returns
347	# success. The caller's `docker push` reads that credential from the file, so
348	# the push completes without ever touching the Windows vault.
349	# Args: token registry_host
350	rbgo_docker_login() {
351	  local -r z_token="${1:?rbgo_docker_login: token required}"
352	  local -r z_host="${2:?rbgo_docker_login: registry host required}"
353	
354	  local z_attempt=0
355	  local z_stderr_file=""
356	  local z_rc=0
357	  local z_auth_b64=""
358	
359	  while :; do
360	    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
361	    z_stderr_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_docker_login_${z_attempt}_stderr.txt"
362	
363	    z_rc=0
364	    printf '%s' "${z_token}" \
365	      | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_host}" \
366	          > /dev/null 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
367	      || z_rc=$?
368	
369	    test "${z_rc}" -ne 0 || break
370	
371	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"
372	
373	    # Palisade bend (docker's own credential store): under headless Cygwin the
374	    # wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for, and
375	    # an empty credsStore does not divert Windows docker to the file store
376	    # (verified — the CLI still detects wincred). Auth already succeeded, so
377	    # write the credential into the base64 file store ourselves — the on-disk
378	    # `auths` form WSL uses and `docker push` reads directly — and treat login
379	    # as done. credsStore is intentionally omitted so retrieval reads the file.
380	    # A real auth failure emits "unauthorized", never matches, and falls through
381	    # to the surveyed-transient / fail-fast handling below.
382	    if [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE}"* ]]; then
383	      buc_warn "Docker wincred helper cannot persist headless (no interactive Windows logon); writing the authenticated credential to the base64 file store in \${HOME}/.docker/config.json. REMOVE this bend when the host gains a working credential vault."
384	      z_auth_b64=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "oauth2accesstoken:${z_token}") \
385	        || buc_die "Cannot base64-encode the docker credential for the file-store bend"
386	      mkdir -p "${HOME}/.docker" \
387	        || buc_die "Cannot create ${HOME}/.docker for the credential-store bend"
388	      printf '{"auths":{"%s":{"auth":"%s"}}}' "${z_host}" "${z_auth_b64}" > "${HOME}/.docker/config.json" \
389	        || buc_die "Cannot write ${HOME}/.docker/config.json credential-store bend"
390	      return 0
391	    fi
392	
393	    [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE}"* ]] \
394	      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"
395	
396	    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" \
397	      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed after ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts (transient daemon->registry timeout, moby#44350) — see ${z_stderr_file}"
398	
399	    buc_warn "Docker login transient (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}, moby#44350 timeout) — retrying in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
400	    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
401	  done
402	}
403	
404	# eof
405

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Google OAuth - Service account JWT authentication

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGO_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGO_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgo_*)

zrbgo_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgo already kindled"

  # Validate required tools (rehomed from rbl_Locator.sh)
  command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "openssl not found - required for JWT signing and encoding"
  command -v curl    >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "curl not found - required for OAuth exchange"
  command -v jq      >/dev/null 2>&1 || buc_die "jq not found - required for JSON parsing"

  buc_log_args "Ensure RBGC is kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Check environment"
  zburd_sentinel

  buc_log_args "Set Module Variables (ZRBGO_*)"
  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_header.json"
  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_claims.json"
  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_unsigned.txt"
  readonly ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jwt_signature.txt"
  readonly ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_oauth_response.json"
  readonly ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_openssl_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_curl_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_jq_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_consumer_retry_body.json"

  readonly ZRBGO_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgo_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGO_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgo not kindled - call zrbgo_kindle first"
}

######################################################################
# Base64 primitives — generic openssl wrappers.
#
# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of
# kindle order. rbgo base64url-encodes JWT material; rbgg/rbgp decode SA
# private keys and rbfd decodes Cloud Build step outputs. The -A flag
# (suppress line wrapping) is load-bearing on every site and lives only
# here, so it cannot be silently dropped on one path.

rbgo_base64_decode_string_to_file() {
  local -r z_b64="${1:-}"
  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_b64}"    || return 1
  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
  printf '%s' "${z_b64}" | openssl enc -base64 -d -A > "${z_output}" || return 1
}

rbgo_base64_decode_file_to_file() {
  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
  local -r z_output="${2:-}"
  test -n "${z_input}"  || return 1
  test -n "${z_output}" || return 1
  test -f "${z_input}"  || return 1
  openssl enc -base64 -d -A < "${z_input}" > "${z_output}" || return 1
}

rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture() {
  local -r z_input="${1:-}"
  printf '%s' "${z_input}" | openssl enc -base64 -A
}

rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture() {
  local -r z_file="${1:-}"
  test -f "${z_file}" || return 1
  openssl enc -base64 -A < "${z_file}"
}

# Stateless — no sentinel; safe to call from any module regardless of kindle order.
rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate() {
  case "${1:-}" in
    7|28|35|56) return 0 ;;
    *)          return 1 ;;
  esac
}

zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture() {
  zrbgo_sentinel

  local -r z_input="${1:-}"

  # Base64url: encode, then URL-safe transform and strip = padding
  local z_encoded
  z_encoded=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "${z_input}") || {
    buc_warn "openssl base64url encode failed"
    return 1
  }
  z_encoded="${z_encoded//+/-}"
  z_encoded="${z_encoded//\//_}"
  z_encoded="${z_encoded//=/}"
  printf '%s' "${z_encoded}"
}

zrbgo_build_jwt_capture() {
  zrbgo_sentinel

  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"

  # RBRA_* expected: CLIENT_EMAIL, PRIVATE_KEY, TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC
  # RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY is sourced as a multi-line string (real newlines from PEM).
  # printf '%b' below is defensive and tolerates either real-newline or '\n'-escape
  # form; PEM keys contain no backslashes so %b is otherwise a no-op.
  # Only source if not already loaded (avoids readonly conflict in subshells)
  if test -z "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"; then
    buc_log_args "Source RBRA file"
    source "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1
  fi

  buc_log_args "Validate required variables"
  test -n "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL:-}"       || return 1
  test -n "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY:-}"        || return 1
  test -n "${RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC:-}" || return 1

  buc_log_args "Build JWT header"
  printf '%s' '{"alg":"RS256","typ":"JWT"}' > "${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}"

  buc_log_args "Calculate timestamps"
  local z_now
  z_now=$(date +%s) || return 1
  local -r z_exp=$((z_now + RBRA_TOKEN_LIFETIME_SEC))

  buc_log_args "Build JWT claims"
  jq -n                                         \
    --arg iss   "${RBRA_CLIENT_EMAIL}"          \
    --arg scope "${RBGC_SCOPE_CLOUD_PLATFORM}"  \
    --arg aud   "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"       \
    --argjson iat "${z_now}"                    \
    --argjson exp "${z_exp}"                    \
    '{iss: $iss, scope: $scope, aud: $aud, iat: $iat, exp: $exp}' \
    > "${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}" || return 1

  buc_log_args "Encode header and claims"
  local z_header_enc
  z_header_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_HEADER_FILE}")") || return 1

  local z_claims_enc
  z_claims_enc=$(zrbgo_base64url_encode_capture "$(<"${ZRBGO_JWT_CLAIMS_FILE}")") || return 1

  buc_log_args "Create unsigned JWT"
  printf '%s' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}" > "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}"

  buc_log_args "Sign with RSA256 (without writing key to disk)"
  # Convert literal \n sequences to real newlines via printf %b and feed via process substitution
  openssl dgst -sha256 \
    -sign <(printf '%b' "${RBRA_PRIVATE_KEY}\n") \
    -out "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}" \
    "${ZRBGO_JWT_UNSIGNED_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}" || {
      buc_warn "openssl RSA256 sign failed: $(<"${ZRBGO_OPENSSL_STDERR_FILE}")"
      return 1
    }

  buc_log_args "Base64url encode signature"
  local z_signature
  z_signature=$(rbgo_base64_encode_file_capture "${ZRBGO_JWT_SIGNATURE_FILE}") || {
    buc_warn "openssl signature base64 encode failed"
    return 1
  }
  z_signature="${z_signature//+/-}"
  z_signature="${z_signature//\//_}"
  z_signature="${z_signature//=/}"

  buc_log_args "Return complete JWT"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_header_enc}.${z_claims_enc}.${z_signature}"
}

zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture() {
  zrbgo_sentinel

  local -r z_jwt="$1"

  local z_attempt=0
  local z_curl_status=0
  while :; do
    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))

    buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
    z_curl_status=0
    curl -sS -X POST "${RBGC_OAUTH_TOKEN_URL}"                                        \
      --connect-timeout "${RBCC_CURL_CONNECT_TIMEOUT_SEC}"                           \
      --max-time "${RBCC_CURL_MAX_TIME_SEC}"                                         \
      -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded"                           \
      -d "grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=${z_jwt}" \
      > "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}" || z_curl_status=$?

    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 && break

    buc_warn "OAuth curl exit ${z_curl_status}: $(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")"
    rbgo_curl_status_is_transient_predicate "${z_curl_status}" || return 1
    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" || return 1

    buc_step "OAuth token mint: transient curl ${z_curl_status}, retry ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
  done

  # Scrubber filters by field NAME, not value. Deliberate best-effort log hygiene:
  # explicitly deletes access_token/refresh_token, then drops any field whose key
  # matches token|secret|key|password (case-insensitive) — catches id_token,
  # client_secret, etc. If the OAuth provider ever returns a new secret-carrying
  # field whose name doesn't match this regex, the scrub would miss it; update
  # the regex here when that happens.
  buc_log_args "Debug: Show the actual response (minus secrets)"
  jq 'del(.access_token, .refresh_token)
      | with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
    "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" | buc_log_pipe || buc_log_args "OAuth response parsing failed"

  buc_log_args "OAuth token exchange completed"

  buc_log_args "Extract access token"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(jq -r '.access_token // empty' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}") || return 1
  test -n "${z_token}" || {
    buc_warn "OAuth response missing access_token; redacted response body follows:"
    jq 'with_entries(select(.key | test("token|secret|key|password"; "i") | not))' \
      "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}" >&2 \
      || printf '%s\n' "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")" >&2
    return 1
  }

  printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgo_*)

rbgo_get_token_capture() {
  zrbgo_sentinel

  local -r z_rbra_file="$1"

  buc_doc_brief "Exchange service account credentials for OAuth2 access token (retries on JWT-propagation race)"
  buc_doc_param "rbra_file" "Path to RBRA credentials file containing RBRA_* variables"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || return 1

  local z_attempt=0
  local z_elapsed=0
  local z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
  local z_jwt=""
  local z_token=""
  local z_status=0
  local z_err=""
  local z_err_desc=""

  while :; do
    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))

    z_status=0
    z_jwt=$(zrbgo_build_jwt_capture "${z_rbra_file}") || z_status=$?
    test "${z_status}" -eq 0 || return 1

    z_status=0
    z_token=$(zrbgo_exchange_jwt_capture "${z_jwt}") || z_status=$?

    if test "${z_status}" -eq 0 && test -n "${z_token}"; then
      printf '%s\n' "${z_token}"
      return 0
    fi

    # TEMP-FORENSIC ₢BBABL: capture every token-endpoint mint failure to a
    # stable, append-only absolute log so a later successful mint cannot clobber
    # the failing body (the memo's Host-B capture gap, memo-20260527). Purpose:
    # confirm the ACCOUNT_STATE_INVALID token-endpoint shape before writing the
    # touchpoint-2 match. REMOVE once the shape is captured and the match lands.
    { printf '=== rbgo mint fail rbra=%s status=%s ===\n' "${z_rbra_file}" "${z_status}"
      printf '%s' "$(<"${ZRBGO_CURL_STDERR_FILE}")" "$(<"${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}")"
      printf '\n=== end ===\n'
    } >> /tmp/rbgo_BBABL_forensic.log 2>/dev/null || true

    # Discriminate failure: only the SA-propagation race shapes retry —
    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid JWT Signature.`         (fresh-key propagation lag)
    #   `invalid_grant` + `Invalid grant: account not found` (fresh-SA propagation lag)
    # Any other shape fails fast.
    z_err=$(jq -r '.error // ""'             "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err=""
    z_err_desc=$(jq -r '.error_description // ""' "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" 2>"${ZRBGO_JQ_STDERR_FILE}") || z_err_desc=""

    test "${z_err}" = "invalid_grant" || return 1
    case "${z_err_desc}" in
      "Invalid JWT Signature."|"Invalid grant: account not found") ;;
      *) return 1 ;;
    esac

    cp "${ZRBGO_OAUTH_RESPONSE_FILE}" "${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}" || return 1

    if test "${z_elapsed}" -ge "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_BUDGET_SEC}"; then
      buc_warn "Token mint: SA propagation race timeout after ${z_elapsed}s (${z_attempt} attempts); last body: ${ZRBGO_CONSUMER_RETRY_BODY_FILE}"
      return 1
    fi

    buc_step "Token mint: attempt ${z_attempt} hit SA propagation race (${z_err_desc}) at ${z_elapsed}s, sleep ${z_delay}s"
    sleep "${z_delay}"
    z_elapsed=$((z_elapsed + z_delay))
    z_delay=$((z_delay * 2))
    test "${z_delay}" -le "${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}" || z_delay="${RBGC_SA_KEY_CONSUMER_RETRY_MAX_DELAY_SEC}"
  done
}

# Authenticate the host docker client to a GAR registry with bounded retry on
# the moby/moby#44350 premature-timeout transient (see
# RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE). docker login is the lone login/pull/push
# verb with no internal retry and a hardcoded 15s daemon->registry auth timeout;
# against a healthy-but-slow backend it fails where the endpoint is in fact fine.
# This mirrors the curl-transient tolerance in rbgu_http_json: retry only the
# surveyed signature, fail fast on everything else (real auth failures emit
# "unauthorized" and do not match), so clean-failure semantics are preserved.
# Stateless (no sentinel) — safe to call from any module regardless of kindle
# order, like rbgu_curl_status_is_transient_predicate.
#
# A second Palisade neighbor lives here for headless Cygwin: Docker Desktop's
# wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for
# (RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE), and Windows docker ignores an empty
# credsStore, so config alone cannot divert the store to the file store. On
# that exact signature the function bends ONCE: auth already succeeded, so it
# writes the credential straight into docker's base64 file store at
# ${HOME}/.docker/config.json (the `auths` map, the form WSL uses) and returns
# success. The caller's `docker push` reads that credential from the file, so
# the push completes without ever touching the Windows vault.
# Args: token registry_host
rbgo_docker_login() {
  local -r z_token="${1:?rbgo_docker_login: token required}"
  local -r z_host="${2:?rbgo_docker_login: registry host required}"

  local z_attempt=0
  local z_stderr_file=""
  local z_rc=0
  local z_auth_b64=""

  while :; do
    z_attempt=$((z_attempt + 1))
    z_stderr_file="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgo_docker_login_${z_attempt}_stderr.txt"

    z_rc=0
    printf '%s' "${z_token}" \
      | docker login -u oauth2accesstoken --password-stdin "https://${z_host}" \
          > /dev/null 2>"${z_stderr_file}" \
      || z_rc=$?

    test "${z_rc}" -ne 0 || break

    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"

    # Palisade bend (docker's own credential store): under headless Cygwin the
    # wincred helper cannot persist the credential auth just succeeded for, and
    # an empty credsStore does not divert Windows docker to the file store
    # (verified — the CLI still detects wincred). Auth already succeeded, so
    # write the credential into the base64 file store ourselves — the on-disk
    # `auths` form WSL uses and `docker push` reads directly — and treat login
    # as done. credsStore is intentionally omitted so retrieval reads the file.
    # A real auth failure emits "unauthorized", never matches, and falls through
    # to the surveyed-transient / fail-fast handling below.
    if [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_WINCRED_HEADLESS_SIGNATURE}"* ]]; then
      buc_warn "Docker wincred helper cannot persist headless (no interactive Windows logon); writing the authenticated credential to the base64 file store in \${HOME}/.docker/config.json. REMOVE this bend when the host gains a working credential vault."
      z_auth_b64=$(rbgo_base64_encode_string_capture "oauth2accesstoken:${z_token}") \
        || buc_die "Cannot base64-encode the docker credential for the file-store bend"
      mkdir -p "${HOME}/.docker" \
        || buc_die "Cannot create ${HOME}/.docker for the credential-store bend"
      printf '{"auths":{"%s":{"auth":"%s"}}}' "${z_host}" "${z_auth_b64}" > "${HOME}/.docker/config.json" \
        || buc_die "Cannot write ${HOME}/.docker/config.json credential-store bend"
      return 0
    fi

    [[ "$(<"${z_stderr_file}")" == *"${RBGC_DOCKER_LOGIN_TRANSIENT_SIGNATURE}"* ]] \
      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}" \
      || buc_die "Docker login to ${z_host} failed after ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts (transient daemon->registry timeout, moby#44350) — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    buc_warn "Docker login transient (attempt ${z_attempt}/${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}, moby#44350 timeout) — retrying in ${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}s"
    sleep "${RBGC_HTTP_TRANSIENT_RETRY_SLEEP_SEC}"
  done
}

buc_log_args() { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_log_args " "$@"; }
buc_log_pipe() { zbuc_make_tag 3 "buc_log_pipe "; zbuc_log "${ZBUC_TAG}" " ---- "; }

buc_step()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_step     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_BRIGHT_WHITE "$*"; }
buc_code()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_code     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 BUYC_CYAN         "$*"; }
buc_info()     { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_info     " "$@"; zbuc_print 0 ""                "$@"; }
buc_debug()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_debug    " "$@"; zbuc_print 2 ""                "$@"; }
buc_trace()    { zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_trace    " "$@"; zbuc_print 3 ""                "$@"; }
buc_warn() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_warn     " "$@"
  buyy_warn_yawp "WARNING:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print 0 "" "${z_pfx} $*"
}
buc_success() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_success  " "$@"
  zbuc_tint BUYC_GREEN "$*"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2 || buc_die
}
# Band membrane: $? captured before any command so a `cmd || buc_die` chain
# whose cmd exited with a precision-band code (bubc band tinder) re-exits
# that code instead of laundering it to 1. Everything else — including a
# cold caller before bubc is sourced — stays exit 1, "imprecise death".
buc_die() {
  local z_status=$?
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_die      " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  if test -n "${BUBC_band_base:-}"                                \
     && test "${z_status}" -ge "${BUBC_band_base}"                \
     && test "${z_status}" -lt "$((BUBC_band_base + BUBC_band_width))"; then
    exit "${z_status}"
  fi
  exit 1
}

# Deliberate rejection origin. Exits with an in-band code from the bubc band
# tinder block so a negative test can assert WHICH gate fired; buc_die
# wrappers upstream propagate the code unchanged through the band membrane.
# An out-of-band argument is a programming error and dies imprecisely.
buc_reject() {
  local z_code="${1:-}"
  shift || true
  test -n "${BUBC_band_base:-}" || buc_die "buc_reject: band tinder not sourced (bubc_constants.sh)"
  test -n "${z_code}"           || buc_die "buc_reject: band code required"
  test "${z_code}" -ge "${BUBC_band_base}" 2>/dev/null || buc_die "buc_reject: code '${z_code}' below band"
  test "${z_code}" -lt "$((BUBC_band_base + BUBC_band_width))" || buc_die "buc_reject: code '${z_code}' above band"
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_reject   " "ERROR: [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  buyy_fail_yawp "ERROR:"; local z_pfx="${z_buym_yelp}"
  zbuc_print -1 "" "${z_pfx} [${ZBUC_CONTEXT:-}] $*"
  exit "${z_code}"
}

# Display unprefixed cyan text for typeable guidance (commands, config values).
# Sigil-less by design — routes color through the core but skips the context tag.
buc_bare() {
  zbuc_tag_args 3 "buc_bare     " "$@"
  zbuc_tint BUYC_CYAN "$*"
  printf '%s\n' "${z_buym_format}" >&2
}

# Display tabtarget hint: resolves colophon to tabtarget filename
# Args: colophon [extra_args...]
buc_tabtarget() {
  local z_colophon="$1"
  shift
  local z_extra="${*:+ $*}"
  zbuym_tt_path "${z_colophon}"
  test -n "${z_buym_tt_path}" || buc_die "buc_tabtarget: no tabtarget found for colophon '${z_colophon}'"
  buc_bare "        ${z_buym_tt_path}${z_extra}"

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
10	#
11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Google Verification - JWT SA and Payor OAuth access verification
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBGV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBGV_SOURCED=1
26	
27	######################################################################
28	# Internal Functions (zrbgv_*)
29	
30	zrbgv_kindle() {
31	  test -z "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv already kindled"
32	
33	  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
34	  zrbgc_sentinel
35	  zrbgo_sentinel
36	  zrbuh_sentinel
37	  zrbgp_sentinel
38	
39	  # Kindle-constant temp file paths for forensic visibility
40	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_resp.json"
41	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_code.txt"
42	  readonly ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_stderr.txt"
43	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_resp.json"
44	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_code.txt"
45	  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_stderr.txt"
46	
47	  # Transient-5xx retry policy: 3 attempts with initial 2s backoff doubling each retry (worst-case +6s)
48	  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
49	  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2
50	
51	  readonly ZRBGV_KINDLED=1
52	}
53	
54	zrbgv_sentinel() {
55	  test "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgv not kindled - call zrbgv_kindle first"
56	}
57	
58	# Convert milliseconds to a decimal seconds string suitable for sleep
59	zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture() {
60	  zrbgv_sentinel
61	
62	  local z_ms="${1}"
63	
64	  buc_log_args "Converting ${z_ms}ms to sleep duration"
65	
66	  # Compute whole seconds and remainder milliseconds using integer arithmetic
67	  local z_sec=$(( z_ms / 1000 ))
68	  local z_rem=$(( z_ms % 1000 ))
69	
70	  # Format as decimal with three fractional digits (sleep accepts e.g. 1.500)
71	  printf '%d.%03d' "${z_sec}" "${z_rem}"
72	}
73	
74	# Resolve the RBRA file path for a given role name
75	# Role names: governor | director | retriever
76	zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture() {
77	  zrbgv_sentinel
78	
79	  local z_role="${1}"
80	
81	  case "${z_role}" in
82	    governor)
83	      test -n "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
84	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}"
85	      ;;
86	    director)
87	      test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
88	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
89	      ;;
90	    retriever)
91	      test -n "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE is not set"
92	      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
93	      ;;
94	    *)
95	      buc_die "Unknown role '${z_role}': expected governor | director | retriever"
96	      ;;
97	  esac
98	}
99	
100	# HTTP GET with bounded exponential-backoff retry on transient 5xx responses.
101	# On any non-5xx response (including auth errors), populates z_code_file and returns.
102	# On curl-network failure or exhausted 5xx retries, buc_die. The caller evaluates
103	# the final HTTP status via its own case-switch on the populated z_code_file.
104	zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry() {
105	  zrbgv_sentinel
106	
107	  local -r z_label="${1}"
108	  local -r z_url="${2}"
109	  local -r z_token="${3}"
110	  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
111	  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
112	  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"
113	
114	  local z_attempt=1
115	  local z_delay="${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
116	  local z_curl_status=0
117	  local z_code=""
118	
119	  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; do
120	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: HTTP GET attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"
121	
122	    z_curl_status=0
123	    rbuh_request "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}"          \
124	                      "${z_resp_file}" "${z_code_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
125	      || z_curl_status=$?
126	
127	    buc_log_args "${z_label}: curl exit status ${z_curl_status}"
128	    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"
129	
130	    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 \
131	      || buc_die "${z_label}: curl failed (network/SSL/DNS) — see ${z_stderr_file}"
132	
133	    z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "${z_label}: failed to read HTTP code file"
134	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "${z_label}: empty HTTP code from curl"
135	
136	    case "${z_code}" in
137	      500|502|503|504)
138	        if test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; then
139	          buc_step "${z_label}: transient HTTP ${z_code}, retrying in ${z_delay}s (attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
140	          sleep "${z_delay}"
141	          z_delay=$(( z_delay * 2 ))
142	          z_attempt=$(( z_attempt + 1 ))
143	        else
144	          buc_die "${z_label}: repeated transient HTTP ${z_code} after ${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts — see ${z_resp_file}"
145	        fi
146	        ;;
147	      *)
148	        return 0
149	        ;;
150	    esac
151	  done
152	}
153	
154	# Extract '.error.message' from a JSON response file for diagnostic surfacing.
155	# Returns a fallback string for empty/missing/malformed bodies. jq stderr captured
156	# to the paired stderr file (no suppression). Exactly one line printed in all paths.
157	zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture() {
158	  zrbgv_sentinel
159	
160	  local -r z_resp_file="${1}"
161	  local -r z_stderr_file="${2}"
162	
163	  test -s "${z_resp_file}" \
164	    || { printf 'empty response body\n'; return 0; }
165	
166	  jq -e . "${z_resp_file}" > /dev/null 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
167	    || { printf 'non-JSON response body — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"; return 0; }
168	
169	  jq -r '.error.message // "no error.message field"' "${z_resp_file}" 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
170	    || printf 'jq extraction failed — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"
171	}
172	
173	# Execute one Artifact Registry packages.list probe call for a JWT SA role.
174	# Pure observation: populates ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE, ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE, ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE
175	# and prints the resulting HTTP code to stdout. Verdict logic lives in the caller
176	# (rbgv_jwt_sa_probe) which has loop context to discriminate retry-on-403 from
177	# fail-fast on other shapes. buc_dies only on infrastructure failures.
178	zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture() {
179	  zrbgv_sentinel
180	
181	  local -r z_role="${1}"
182	  local -r z_attempt="${2}"
183	
184	  buc_log_args "JWT SA probe attempt ${z_attempt} for role '${z_role}'"
185	
186	  buc_log_args "Resolve RBRA file for role"
187	  local z_rbra_file
188	  z_rbra_file=$(zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture "${z_role}") \
189	    || buc_die "Failed to resolve RBRA file for role ${z_role}"
190	
191	  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
192	  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "RBRA file not found for role ${z_role}: ${z_rbra_file}"
193	
194	  buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token"
195	  local z_token
196	  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${z_rbra_file}") \
197	    || buc_die "Failed to obtain OAuth token for role ${z_role} (attempt ${z_attempt})"
198	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty OAuth token for role ${z_role} (attempt ${z_attempt})"
199	
200	  buc_log_args "Build Artifact Registry packages.list URL"
201	  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
202	  test -n "${RBRD_GCP_REGION:-}"       || buc_die "RBRD_GCP_REGION is not set"
203	  test -n "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY:-}"   || buc_die "RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY is not set"
204	
205	  local -r z_ar_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRD_GCP_REGION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/packages"
206	  local -r z_ar_label="JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_attempt}"
207	
208	  buc_log_args "Call Artifact Registry packages.list (with transient-5xx retry)"
209	  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
210	    "${z_ar_label}"             \
211	    "${z_ar_url}"               \
212	    "${z_token}"                \
213	    "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}"     \
214	    "${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}"     \
215	    "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}"
216	
217	  local z_code
218	  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read AR HTTP code file"
219	  test -n "${z_code}"                || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from AR curl"
220	
221	  buc_log_args "AR packages.list HTTP ${z_code} for role ${z_role} attempt ${z_attempt}"
222	
223	  printf '%s\n' "${z_code}"
224	}
225	
226	# Execute one CRM projects.get probe iteration for the Payor OAuth flow.
227	# Writes response to ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE, HTTP code to ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE,
228	# and stderr to ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE (kindle-constant paths for forensic visibility).
229	zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once() {
230	  zrbgv_sentinel
231	
232	  local z_iteration="${1}"
233	
234	  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"
235	
236	  buc_log_args "Authenticate via Payor OAuth refresh token flow"
237	  local z_token
238	  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) \
239	    || buc_die "Failed to obtain Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"
240	  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"
241	
242	  buc_log_args "Build CRM projects.get URL"
243	  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"
244	
245	  local -r z_crm_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}"
246	  local -r z_crm_label="Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"
247	
248	  buc_log_args "Call CRM projects.get on payor project (with transient-5xx retry)"
249	  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
250	    "${z_crm_label}"            \
251	    "${z_crm_url}"              \
252	    "${z_token}"                \
253	    "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}"    \
254	    "${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}"    \
255	    "${ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE}"
256	
257	  local z_code
258	  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read CRM HTTP code file"
259	  test -n "${z_code}"                 || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from CRM curl"
260	
261	  buc_log_args "CRM projects.get HTTP ${z_code} for Payor iteration ${z_iteration}"
262	
263	  case "${z_code}" in
264	    200)
265	      buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
266	      ;;
267	    401|403)
268	      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: access denied (HTTP ${z_code})"
269	      ;;
270	    *)
271	      local z_err=""
272	      if jq -e . "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
273	        z_err=$(jq -r '.error.message // "Unknown error"' "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null) || z_err="Unknown error"
274	      else
275	        z_err="Non-JSON response (HTTP ${z_code})"
276	      fi
277	      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
278	      ;;
279	  esac
280	}
281	
282	######################################################################
283	# External Functions (rbgv_*)
284	
285	# Probe 1: JWT SA Access Probe
286	#
287	# Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 (the
288	# IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature in the post-mint window) retries with delay;
289	# any other HTTP code dies with body summary. Past max attempts on 403, dies with
290	# body summary as 'budget exhausted'. Layers cleanly with rbgo_get_token_capture's
291	# JWT-signature retry: each layer recognizes its own 'not yet' and retries.
292	#
293	# All three roles have read access:
294	#   Governor  = Owner
295	#   Director  = repoAdmin
296	#   Retriever = reader
297	rbgv_jwt_sa_probe() {
298	  zrbgv_sentinel
299	
300	  local -r z_role="${1:-}"
301	  local -r z_count="${2:-1}"
302	  local -r z_delay_ms="${3:-0}"
303	
304	  buc_doc_brief "Probe JWT service account access for a given role against Artifact Registry"
305	  buc_doc_param "role"     "Role to probe: governor | director | retriever"
306	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Maximum attempts; first 2xx wins, retries on HTTP 403 (default: 1)"
307	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between retries (default: 0)"
308	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
309	
310	  test -n "${z_role}"  || buc_die "role parameter required (governor | director | retriever)"
311	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
312	
313	  buc_step "JWT SA access probe: role=${z_role} count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
314	
315	  local z_iter=1
316	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
317	    buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
318	
319	    local z_code
320	    z_code=$(zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture "${z_role}" "${z_iter}") \
321	      || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: probe-once observation failed"
322	    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: empty HTTP code"
323	
324	    case "${z_code}" in
325	      200|206)
326	        buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
327	        return 0
328	        ;;
329	      403)
330	        if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}"; then
331	          buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: 'not yet' (HTTP 403); retrying"
332	          if test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
333	            local z_sleep
334	            z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
335	            buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next attempt"
336	            sleep "${z_sleep}"
337	          fi
338	        else
339	          local z_err
340	          z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
341	          buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: propagation budget exhausted (HTTP 403): ${z_err}"
342	        fi
343	        ;;
344	      *)
345	        local z_err
346	        z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
347	        buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
348	        ;;
349	    esac
350	
351	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
352	  done
353	
354	  buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}]: loop exited without verdict — internal logic error"
355	}
356	
357	# Probe 2: Payor OAuth Access Probe
358	#
359	# For each iteration:
360	#   1. Authenticate via zrbgp_authenticate_capture (RBRO refresh token -> access token)
361	#   2. Call CRM projects.get on payor project to verify token works
362	#   3. Sleep for configured delay
363	rbgv_payor_oauth_probe() {
364	  zrbgv_sentinel
365	
366	  local z_count="${1:-1}"
367	  local z_delay_ms="${2:-0}"
368	
369	  buc_doc_brief "Probe Payor OAuth access against Cloud Resource Manager (CRM) projects.get"
370	  buc_doc_param "count"    "Number of iterations (default: 1)"
371	  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between iterations (default: 0)"
372	  buc_doc_shown || return 0
373	
374	  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"
375	
376	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe: count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"
377	
378	  local z_iter=1
379	  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
380	    buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iter}/${z_count}"
381	
382	    zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once "${z_iter}"
383	
384	    if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}" && test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
385	      local z_sleep
386	      z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
387	      buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next iteration"
388	      sleep "${z_sleep}"
389	    fi
390	
391	    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
392	  done
393	
394	  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe complete: iterations=${z_count}"
395	}
396	
397	# eof
398

#!/bin/bash
#
# Copyright 2026 Scale Invariant, Inc.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
#
# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
#
# Recipe Bottle Google Verification - JWT SA and Payor OAuth access verification

set -euo pipefail

# Multiple inclusion detection
test -z "${ZRBGV_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
ZRBGV_SOURCED=1

######################################################################
# Internal Functions (zrbgv_*)

zrbgv_kindle() {
  test -z "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbgv already kindled"

  buc_log_args "Ensure dependencies are kindled first"
  zrbgc_sentinel
  zrbgo_sentinel
  zrbuh_sentinel
  zrbgp_sentinel

  # Kindle-constant temp file paths for forensic visibility
  readonly ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_resp.json"
  readonly ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_code.txt"
  readonly ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_ar_stderr.txt"
  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_resp.json"
  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_code.txt"
  readonly ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE="${BURD_TEMP_DIR}/rbgv_crm_stderr.txt"

  # Transient-5xx retry policy: 3 attempts with initial 2s backoff doubling each retry (worst-case +6s)
  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS=3
  readonly ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC=2

  readonly ZRBGV_KINDLED=1
}

zrbgv_sentinel() {
  test "${ZRBGV_KINDLED:-}" = "1" || buc_die "Module rbgv not kindled - call zrbgv_kindle first"
}

# Convert milliseconds to a decimal seconds string suitable for sleep
zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_ms="${1}"

  buc_log_args "Converting ${z_ms}ms to sleep duration"

  # Compute whole seconds and remainder milliseconds using integer arithmetic
  local z_sec=$(( z_ms / 1000 ))
  local z_rem=$(( z_ms % 1000 ))

  # Format as decimal with three fractional digits (sleep accepts e.g. 1.500)
  printf '%d.%03d' "${z_sec}" "${z_rem}"
}

# Resolve the RBRA file path for a given role name
# Role names: governor | director | retriever
zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_role="${1}"

  case "${z_role}" in
    governor)
      test -n "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_GOVERNOR_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    director)
      test -n "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE:-}"  || buc_die "RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_DIRECTOR_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    retriever)
      test -n "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE is not set"
      printf '%s\n' "${RBDC_RETRIEVER_RBRA_FILE}"
      ;;
    *)
      buc_die "Unknown role '${z_role}': expected governor | director | retriever"
      ;;
  esac
}

# HTTP GET with bounded exponential-backoff retry on transient 5xx responses.
# On any non-5xx response (including auth errors), populates z_code_file and returns.
# On curl-network failure or exhausted 5xx retries, buc_die. The caller evaluates
# the final HTTP status via its own case-switch on the populated z_code_file.
zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local -r z_label="${1}"
  local -r z_url="${2}"
  local -r z_token="${3}"
  local -r z_resp_file="${4}"
  local -r z_code_file="${5}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${6}"

  local z_attempt=1
  local z_delay="${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_INITIAL_DELAY_SEC}"
  local z_curl_status=0
  local z_code=""

  while test "${z_attempt}" -le "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; do
    buc_log_args "${z_label}: HTTP GET attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"

    z_curl_status=0
    rbuh_request "GET" "${z_url}" "${z_token}"          \
                      "${z_resp_file}" "${z_code_file}" "${z_stderr_file}" \
      || z_curl_status=$?

    buc_log_args "${z_label}: curl exit status ${z_curl_status}"
    buc_log_pipe < "${z_stderr_file}"

    test "${z_curl_status}" -eq 0 \
      || buc_die "${z_label}: curl failed (network/SSL/DNS) — see ${z_stderr_file}"

    z_code=$(<"${z_code_file}") || buc_die "${z_label}: failed to read HTTP code file"
    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "${z_label}: empty HTTP code from curl"

    case "${z_code}" in
      500|502|503|504)
        if test "${z_attempt}" -lt "${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS}"; then
          buc_step "${z_label}: transient HTTP ${z_code}, retrying in ${z_delay}s (attempt ${z_attempt}/${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS})"
          sleep "${z_delay}"
          z_delay=$(( z_delay * 2 ))
          z_attempt=$(( z_attempt + 1 ))
        else
          buc_die "${z_label}: repeated transient HTTP ${z_code} after ${ZRBGV_HTTP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS} attempts — see ${z_resp_file}"
        fi
        ;;
      *)
        return 0
        ;;
    esac
  done
}

# Extract '.error.message' from a JSON response file for diagnostic surfacing.
# Returns a fallback string for empty/missing/malformed bodies. jq stderr captured
# to the paired stderr file (no suppression). Exactly one line printed in all paths.
zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local -r z_resp_file="${1}"
  local -r z_stderr_file="${2}"

  test -s "${z_resp_file}" \
    || { printf 'empty response body\n'; return 0; }

  jq -e . "${z_resp_file}" > /dev/null 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
    || { printf 'non-JSON response body — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"; return 0; }

  jq -r '.error.message // "no error.message field"' "${z_resp_file}" 2>>"${z_stderr_file}" \
    || printf 'jq extraction failed — see %s\n' "${z_stderr_file}"
}

# Execute one Artifact Registry packages.list probe call for a JWT SA role.
# Pure observation: populates ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE, ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE, ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE
# and prints the resulting HTTP code to stdout. Verdict logic lives in the caller
# (rbgv_jwt_sa_probe) which has loop context to discriminate retry-on-403 from
# fail-fast on other shapes. buc_dies only on infrastructure failures.
zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local -r z_role="${1}"
  local -r z_attempt="${2}"

  buc_log_args "JWT SA probe attempt ${z_attempt} for role '${z_role}'"

  buc_log_args "Resolve RBRA file for role"
  local z_rbra_file
  z_rbra_file=$(zrbgv_role_rbra_file_capture "${z_role}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to resolve RBRA file for role ${z_role}"

  buc_log_args "Validate RBRA file exists"
  test -f "${z_rbra_file}" || buc_die "RBRA file not found for role ${z_role}: ${z_rbra_file}"

  buc_log_args "Exchange JWT for OAuth token"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(rbgo_get_token_capture "${z_rbra_file}") \
    || buc_die "Failed to obtain OAuth token for role ${z_role} (attempt ${z_attempt})"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty OAuth token for role ${z_role} (attempt ${z_attempt})"

  buc_log_args "Build Artifact Registry packages.list URL"
  test -n "${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID is not set"
  test -n "${RBRD_GCP_REGION:-}"       || buc_die "RBRD_GCP_REGION is not set"
  test -n "${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY:-}"   || buc_die "RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY is not set"

  local -r z_ar_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_ARTIFACTREGISTRY}${RBGC_ARTIFACTREGISTRY_V1}/projects/${RBDC_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID}/locations/${RBRD_GCP_REGION}/repositories/${RBDC_GAR_REPOSITORY}/packages"
  local -r z_ar_label="JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_attempt}"

  buc_log_args "Call Artifact Registry packages.list (with transient-5xx retry)"
  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
    "${z_ar_label}"             \
    "${z_ar_url}"               \
    "${z_token}"                \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}"     \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}"     \
    "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}"

  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_AR_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read AR HTTP code file"
  test -n "${z_code}"                || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from AR curl"

  buc_log_args "AR packages.list HTTP ${z_code} for role ${z_role} attempt ${z_attempt}"

  printf '%s\n' "${z_code}"
}

# Execute one CRM projects.get probe iteration for the Payor OAuth flow.
# Writes response to ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE, HTTP code to ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE,
# and stderr to ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE (kindle-constant paths for forensic visibility).
zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_iteration="${1}"

  buc_log_args "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"

  buc_log_args "Authenticate via Payor OAuth refresh token flow"
  local z_token
  z_token=$(zrbgp_authenticate_capture) \
    || buc_die "Failed to obtain Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"
  test -n "${z_token}" || buc_die "Empty Payor OAuth access token (iteration ${z_iteration})"

  buc_log_args "Build CRM projects.get URL"
  test -n "${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID:-}" || buc_die "RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID is not set"

  local -r z_crm_url="${RBGC_API_ROOT_CRM}${RBGC_CRM_V1}/projects/${RBRP_PAYOR_PROJECT_ID}"
  local -r z_crm_label="Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}"

  buc_log_args "Call CRM projects.get on payor project (with transient-5xx retry)"
  zrbgv_http_get_with_5xx_retry \
    "${z_crm_label}"            \
    "${z_crm_url}"              \
    "${z_token}"                \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}"    \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}"    \
    "${ZRBGV_CRM_STDERR_FILE}"

  local z_code
  z_code=$(<"${ZRBGV_CRM_CODE_FILE}") || buc_die "Failed to read CRM HTTP code file"
  test -n "${z_code}"                 || buc_die "Empty HTTP code from CRM curl"

  buc_log_args "CRM projects.get HTTP ${z_code} for Payor iteration ${z_iteration}"

  case "${z_code}" in
    200)
      buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    401|403)
      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: access denied (HTTP ${z_code})"
      ;;
    *)
      local z_err=""
      if jq -e . "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        z_err=$(jq -r '.error.message // "Unknown error"' "${ZRBGV_CRM_RESP_FILE}" 2>/dev/null) || z_err="Unknown error"
      else
        z_err="Non-JSON response (HTTP ${z_code})"
      fi
      buc_die "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iteration}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
      ;;
  esac
}

######################################################################
# External Functions (rbgv_*)

# Probe 1: JWT SA Access Probe
#
# Bounded propagation-absorbing retry. First HTTP 200/206 wins; HTTP 403 (the
# IAM-policy-read 'not yet' signature in the post-mint window) retries with delay;
# any other HTTP code dies with body summary. Past max attempts on 403, dies with
# body summary as 'budget exhausted'. Layers cleanly with rbgo_get_token_capture's
# JWT-signature retry: each layer recognizes its own 'not yet' and retries.
#
# All three roles have read access:
#   Governor  = Owner
#   Director  = repoAdmin
#   Retriever = reader
rbgv_jwt_sa_probe() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local -r z_role="${1:-}"
  local -r z_count="${2:-1}"
  local -r z_delay_ms="${3:-0}"

  buc_doc_brief "Probe JWT service account access for a given role against Artifact Registry"
  buc_doc_param "role"     "Role to probe: governor | director | retriever"
  buc_doc_param "count"    "Maximum attempts; first 2xx wins, retries on HTTP 403 (default: 1)"
  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between retries (default: 0)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_role}"  || buc_die "role parameter required (governor | director | retriever)"
  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"

  buc_step "JWT SA access probe: role=${z_role} count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"

  local z_iter=1
  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
    buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}/${z_count}"

    local z_code
    z_code=$(zrbgv_jwt_ar_probe_once_capture "${z_role}" "${z_iter}") \
      || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: probe-once observation failed"
    test -n "${z_code}" || buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: empty HTTP code"

    case "${z_code}" in
      200|206)
        buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: OK (HTTP ${z_code})"
        return 0
        ;;
      403)
        if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}"; then
          buc_step "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: 'not yet' (HTTP 403); retrying"
          if test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
            local z_sleep
            z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
            buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next attempt"
            sleep "${z_sleep}"
          fi
        else
          local z_err
          z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
          buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: propagation budget exhausted (HTTP 403): ${z_err}"
        fi
        ;;
      *)
        local z_err
        z_err=$(zrbgv_jq_error_message_capture "${ZRBGV_AR_RESP_FILE}" "${ZRBGV_AR_STDERR_FILE}")
        buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}] attempt ${z_iter}: unexpected HTTP ${z_code}: ${z_err}"
        ;;
    esac

    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
  done

  buc_die "JWT SA probe [${z_role}]: loop exited without verdict — internal logic error"
}

# Probe 2: Payor OAuth Access Probe
#
# For each iteration:
#   1. Authenticate via zrbgp_authenticate_capture (RBRO refresh token -> access token)
#   2. Call CRM projects.get on payor project to verify token works
#   3. Sleep for configured delay
rbgv_payor_oauth_probe() {
  zrbgv_sentinel

  local z_count="${1:-1}"
  local z_delay_ms="${2:-0}"

  buc_doc_brief "Probe Payor OAuth access against Cloud Resource Manager (CRM) projects.get"
  buc_doc_param "count"    "Number of iterations (default: 1)"
  buc_doc_param "delay_ms" "Milliseconds to sleep between iterations (default: 0)"
  buc_doc_shown || return 0

  test -n "${z_count}" || buc_die "count parameter required"

  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe: count=${z_count} delay_ms=${z_delay_ms}"

  local z_iter=1
  while test "${z_iter}" -le "${z_count}"; do
    buc_step "Payor OAuth probe iteration ${z_iter}/${z_count}"

    zrbgv_payor_crm_probe_once "${z_iter}"

    if test "${z_iter}" -lt "${z_count}" && test "${z_delay_ms}" -gt 0; then
      local z_sleep
      z_sleep=$(zrbgv_ms_to_sleep_capture "${z_delay_ms}")
      buc_log_args "Sleeping ${z_sleep}s (${z_delay_ms}ms) before next iteration"
      sleep "${z_sleep}"
    fi

    z_iter=$(( z_iter + 1 ))
  done

  buc_step "Payor OAuth access probe complete: iterations=${z_count}"
}

1	#!/bin/bash
2	#
3	# Copyright 2025 Scale Invariant, Inc.
4	#
5	# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
6	# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
7	# You may obtain a copy of the License at
8	#
9	#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

11	# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
12	# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
13	# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
14	# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
15	# limitations under the License.
16	#
17	# Author: Brad Hyslop <bhyslop@scaleinvariant.org>
18	#
19	# Recipe Bottle Common Constants - File paths and naming conventions
20	
21	set -euo pipefail
22	
23	# Multiple inclusion detection
24	test -z "${ZRBCC_SOURCED:-}" || buc_die "Module rbcc multiply sourced - check sourcing hierarchy"
25	ZRBCC_SOURCED=1
26	
27	# Kit directory — source-time self-location. rbcc lives at the RBK kit root,
28	# so its own directory IS the kit dir. No launcher environment dependency;
29	# available the instant this file is sourced (BCG kit-self-location pattern,
30	# canon: rbtd/rbte_cli.sh).
31	readonly RBCC_KIT_DIR="${BASH_SOURCE[0]%/*}"
32	
33	# Generated zipper-derived artifacts — the theurge build materializes both
34	# (write-on-change), rbq's gates verify them. Absolute, composed from the
35	# readonly kit dir (source-time, no kindle dependency).
36	readonly RBCC_rbtdgc_consts_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/rbtd/src/rbtdgc_consts.rs"
37	readonly RBCC_tabtarget_context_file="${RBCC_KIT_DIR}/claude-rbk-tabtarget-context.md"
38	
39	# ── Moorings inventory constants ──────────────────────────────────────────
40	# Moorings-relative path values. Every consumer reads these names directly
41	# (the transitional RBBC_* aliases were retired by the literal-sweep pace).
42	# Source-time literals, no kindle dependency.
43	RBCC_moorings_dir="rbmm_moorings"
44	RBCC_launchers_subdir="rbml_launchers"
45	RBCC_users_subdir="rbmu_users"
46	RBCC_nodes_subdir="rbmn_nodes"
47	RBCC_vessels_subdir="rbmv_vessels"
48	RBCC_rbrr_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrr.env"
49	RBCC_rbrp_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrp.env"
50	RBCC_rbrm_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/rbrm.env"
51	RBCC_rbrd_basename="rbrd.env"
52	RBCC_rbrd_file="${RBCC_moorings_dir}/${RBCC_rbrd_basename}"
53	
54	# Literal constants (pure string literals, no variable expansion — available at source time)
55	RBCC_rbrs_file="../station-files/rbrs.env"
56	RBCC_rbrn_file="rbrn.env"
57	RBCC_rbra_file="rbra.env"
58	RBCC_rbro_file="rbro.env"
59	# Auth-role enum (rbnae_): the RBRA_ROLE value — minted sprue, RBRA_ROLE domain
60	# (3 members). Written into credential files, validated, carried as the auth
61	# folio, swizzle-checked against BUZ_FOLIO. Distinct axis from RBCC_account_*
62	# below: that one is the bare composition label.
63	RBCC_role_governor="rbnae_governor"
64	RBCC_role_retriever="rbnae_retriever"
65	RBCC_role_director="rbnae_director"
66	
67	# Account composition labels — bare fragments that compose GCP SA account-ids/
68	# emails AND local secret-directory names (6 members, superset of the 3 roles
69	# plus payor/assay/mason which are NEVER RBRA_ROLE values). These stay bare:
70	# a derived resource-name string in cloud/filesystem space, structurally like
71	# the SA email and the -tether/-airgap pool suffixes the heat keeps bare.
72	RBCC_account_governor="governor"
73	RBCC_account_retriever="retriever"
74	RBCC_account_director="director"
75	RBCC_account_payor="payor"
76	RBCC_account_assay="assay"
77	RBCC_account_mason="mason"
78	RBCC_onboarding_nameplate="tadmor"
79	
80	# Operation-verb tinder — the canonical bash home for RBK operation verbs.
81	# Members are bare verb tokens; the group carries one author here so it is
82	# projectable under the single-canonical-author rule. Two surfaces:
83	#
84	#   SA-management (divest/invest/roster) — composed into the fact-extension
85	#   constants below; consumed by the governor/director account surface.
86	RBCC_verb_divest="divest"
87	RBCC_verb_invest="invest"
88	RBCC_verb_roster="roster"
89	#
90	#   Image/build lifecycle (anoint/inscribe/kludge/ordain/yoke) — name the
91	#   registry and build operations. Previously implicit in command-function
92	#   names (rbrd_inscribe, rbfd_ordain, …) and tabtarget descriptions; homed
93	#   here so the group has a single owner rather than being reconstructed by
94	#   grep across rbfd_/rbfl_/rbfk_/rbob_.
95	RBCC_verb_anoint="anoint"
96	RBCC_verb_inscribe="inscribe"
97	RBCC_verb_kludge="kludge"
98	RBCC_verb_ordain="ordain"
99	RBCC_verb_yoke="yoke"
100	
101	# Fact-file extension tinder — multi-fact registry for buf_write_fact_multi.
102	# Producers emit "<basename>.<extension>" via filesystem-as-data-bus pattern;
103	# consumers walk fact files in BURD_OUTPUT_DIR / BURD_TEMP_DIR keyed on extension.
104	# Roster extensions composed from earlier tinder (BCG tinder-on-tinder).
105	RBCC_fact_ext_depot="depot"
106	RBCC_fact_ext_depot_project="depot-project"
107	RBCC_fact_ext_roster_retriever="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_retriever}"
108	RBCC_fact_ext_roster_director="${RBCC_verb_roster}-${RBCC_account_director}"
109	RBCC_fact_ext_audit_hallmark="audit-hallmark"
110	
111	# Container-role tinder — the canonical bash home for the crucible's container
112	# roles. Bare role tokens; the crucible is sentry + pentacle + bottle and every
113	# container name / compose service derives from these. Distinct from the
114	# credential auth-role axis above, which itself splits in two: RBCC_role_*
115	# (minted enum, the RBRA_ROLE value) and RBCC_account_* (bare composition label
116	# for SA names + secret dirs). None of these words are reused across families,
117	# keeping each token monosemous.
118	RBCC_container_bottle="bottle"
119	RBCC_container_pentacle="pentacle"
120	RBCC_container_sentry="sentry"
121	
122	######################################################################
123	# Rust const projection (rbcc single-homed set → RBTDGC_)
124	
125	# rbcc_emit_consts() - Emit the RBCC-owned co-maintained constants as Rust
126	# string consts to stdout, one `pub const` line per name/value pair via the
127	# shared buz_emit_const primitive (BUK must be kindled). The single-homed set:
128	# moorings/vessels dirs, account labels, .env filenames, operation verbs, and
129	# container roles. Each Rust const is
130	# RBTDGC_ + the RBCC stem (RBCC_ prefix stripped) uppercased; the value is
131	# carried verbatim. Bash stays mixed-case (RBCC_moorings_dir); the generated
132	# Rust is SCREAMING (RBTDGC_MOORINGS_DIR) per Rust convention — that casing is
133	# the sole transform, applied mechanically, so there is no per-entry mapping
134	# and no drift. rbtd's lib.rs paths, the manifest account-label mirror, and the
135	# rbtdrk/rbtdrp .env consts all source these instead of hand-copying.
136	# A second section projects the BUBC precision exit-code band as i32 consts
137	# (same mechanical transform, BUBC_ prefix stripped) — theurge asserts exit
138	# codes as integers. bubc is sourced by the launcher on every dispatch, so the
139	# band values are present at emission time with no cross-module tinder trick.
140	rbcc_emit_consts() {
141	  printf '%s\n' "// RBCC constants (rbcc_constants.sh single-homed set)"
142	
143	  local z_name=""
144	  local z_stem=""
145	  local z_upper=""
146	  for z_name in \
147	    RBCC_moorings_dir    \
148	    RBCC_vessels_subdir  \
149	    RBCC_account_governor   \
150	    RBCC_account_retriever  \
151	    RBCC_account_director   \
152	    RBCC_account_payor      \
153	    RBCC_account_assay      \
154	    RBCC_account_mason      \
155	    RBCC_rbrr_file       \
156	    RBCC_rbrp_file       \
157	    RBCC_rbrm_file       \
158	    RBCC_rbrd_basename   \
159	    RBCC_rbrd_file       \
